focus: Verify implementation-summary/resource-map claims against disk state, runtime command mirrors, and advisor rename references

findingDetails.title: Resource map marks nonexistent runtime command mirrors as OK
findingDetails.evidence: resource-map.md claims Missing on disk: 0 and marks Codex command files plus Gemini YAML command assets as OK, while disk inventory shows .codex/commands/deep is absent and .gemini/commands/deep contains only README.txt and improve-agent.toml; implementation-summary.md lines 87-88 already document those shapes correctly.
findingDetails.recommendation: Revise resource-map runtime command mirror rows and summary so Gemini YAML assets and Codex command files are N/A/absent rather than OK.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Direct disk inventory and resource-map rows show the mismatch is confined to runtime command mirror evidence rows for Gemini assets and Codex commands.

noveltyJustification: First correctness iteration found one new P1 evidence-matrix mismatch; no prior iteration findings existed.

traceabilityChecks.notes: Checked implementation-summary/resource-map runtime mirror claims against disk and sampled advisor source rename references.

edgeCases: Resource map appears partly authored as a planning inventory; classified active because packet treats it as first-class release evidence.
ruledOut: Absent .codex/commands/deep is not a runtime implementation failure because implementation-summary documents it as N/A.
Absent Gemini YAML assets are not a runtime implementation failure because implementation-summary documents no YAML assets in that dir.

focus: Security review of renamed deep-agent-improvement scripts and command YAML shell/path handling

findingDetails.title: Unquoted workflow placeholders allow shell/path injection before script validation
findingDetails.evidence: User-controlled spec_folder and target_path placeholders are interpolated unquoted into auto and confirm workflow shell commands before Node scripts can parse or normalize paths.
findingDetails.recommendation: Route user fields through argv arrays or a quoting/escaping helper and enforce canonical spec_folder and .opencode/agents/*.md path normalization before constructing shell commands.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Same unquoted placeholder pattern appears in both auto and confirm workflow command surfaces; reviewed path-consuming scripts parse argv after shell interpolation.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 security finding in a previously unreviewed dimension; no refinements-only findings counted.
edgeCases: Dispatcher may escape placeholders out-of-band; no guarantee was present in reviewed packet, so severity remains P1 with downgrade trigger.
Runtime command-dispatch implementation was not expanded beyond declared representative files.
ruledOut: No active P0 because exploitability depends on command dispatcher escaping behavior.
No separate finding for broad SKILL.md allowed tools because packet-local writes and Bash are expected for this workflow.

focus: Traceability cross-check of spec requirements, task/checklist claims, implementation evidence, and resource-map coverage

findingDetails.title: Completion status is claimed while mandatory memory-save and checklist/task evidence remain pending
findingDetails.evidence: implementation-summary.md claims COMPLETE and all P0/P1 requirements met while spec.md REQ-015 requires /memory:save, implementation-summary.md marks REQ-015 PENDING, tasks.md leaves T-041 unchecked, and checklist.md leaves CHK-055 plus the verification summary unchecked.
findingDetails.recommendation: Downgrade the completion claim until /memory:save is executed and T-041/CHK-055 are checked with evidence, or document a user-approved deferral and remove the blanket all-P0/P1-met statement.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Cross-document packet evidence ties the same REQ-015 requirement through spec.md, implementation-summary.md, tasks.md, and checklist.md; no broader source scan is required for this traceability gate mismatch.

noveltyJustification: One new P1 traceability finding in a previously unreviewed dimension; prior resource-map P1 was carried forward but not counted as new.
edgeCases: completion_pct may mean implementation-only progress, but release wording says all P0/P1 requirements are met while REQ-015 is pending
findings registry is stale/empty relative to JSONL prior findings, so state log and dispatcher summary were used for continuity
resource-map P1-001 carried forward rather than duplicated
integrationEvidence: .gemini/commands/deep/ exact glob returned README.txt and improve-agent.toml only
resource-map runtime rows compared against implementation-summary N/A claims

focus: Maintainability review of naming consistency, documentation clarity, historical-record boundaries, and operator follow-on clarity

findingDetails.title: Install-guide skill inventory still advertises retired sk-deep-* skill IDs
findingDetails.evidence: SET-UP - AGENTS.md active Current Installation table lists sk-deep-research and sk-deep-review, while adjacent current setup surfaces list deep-research and deep-review.
findingDetails.recommendation: Update the SET-UP - AGENTS.md skill inventory to use deep-research and deep-review so operators copying setup tables do not propagate retired skill IDs.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact Grep/Read evidence found stale names in one install-guide table while root README and install-guide summary surfaces already use current names; separate from prior resource-map, shell placeholder, and completion-status P1s.

noveltyJustification: One new maintainability P2 in a previously unreviewed dimension; no P0/P1 findings and no duplicate of prior active P1s.
edgeCases: Allowed historical mentions were not treated as active findings when packet docs framed them as factual history.
Stale sk-deep-research/sk-deep-review install-guide table is broader maintainability drift, not a duplicate of 079 rename findings.
Existing P1-001, P1-002, and P1-003 remain active but were not duplicated.
ruledOut: No new P1 for deep-agent-improvement root/install-guide references; sampled current surfaces use the new skill name.
No finding for retained @improve-agent or /deep:start-agent-improvement-loop because the packet explicitly keeps those stable.
No duplicate P1 for checklist/task/implementation-summary completion mismatch; P1-003 already covers that traceability gate.
